extracted.brandBackground: Learning Express operates retail specialty toy and gift stores featuring toys, books, games, and customer-centric services.

leadership.name: Lauren Derse
leadership.role: Chief Executive Officer, Secretary and Director
leadership.background: Joined Learning Express in June 2012 and has served as CEO since July 2022.
leadership.whyItMatters: Leads the overall strategic direction and brand management of the franchise system.

leadership.name: Kathy Troknya
leadership.background: Joined Learning Express in 1990 and has served as President since November 2011.
leadership.whyItMatters: Oversees all daily franchise operations and system-wide execution.

leadership.name: Mike Derse
leadership.background: Joined in 2012, promoted to CDO in July 2022, and appointed Treasurer in January 2023.
leadership.whyItMatters: Manages franchise development, expansion strategies, and financial treasury.

leadership.name: Kim Schneiderbauer
leadership.background: Joined in 1999 and promoted to Vice President of Finance & HR in January 2018.
leadership.whyItMatters: Directs the financial administration and human resource policies of the franchisor.

cohorts.basis: Average sales of 77 franchised stores open for one full-year or more in 2025.

cohorts.basis: Average sales of 83 franchised stores open for one full-year or more in 2023.

item19.notes: Excludes stores open less than 12 months and one location in Fremont, NE due to population criteria.

lineItems.notes: Standard initial fee for a new store.

flatMonthlyFees.name: Email, Social Media, Text and Loyalty Marketing Service

hiddenCosts.name: Payment by Credit Card Fee
hiddenCosts.description: A fee of 3.5% of the invoice is charged for payments made to the franchisor via credit card.

hiddenCosts.name: POS and Back-Office Computer Maintenance
hiddenCosts.description: Estimated annual cost of licensing, maintaining, and upgrading the POS and back-office computer system is $1,800 to $3,600.

operationalRisks.title: Out-of-State Dispute Resolution
operationalRisks.description: The franchise agreement requires mediation, arbitration, and litigation to take place in Massachusetts.

operationalRisks.title: Mandatory Minimum Royalty Payments
operationalRisks.description: Franchisees must pay a minimum royalty of $1,500 per month regardless of sales performance.

operationalRisks.title: Spousal Personal Guarantee
operationalRisks.description: Spouses or domestic partners of owners with a 5% or greater interest must sign a personal guarantee.

extracted.conceptRationale: Learning Express is a specialty retail toy and gift store concept.
extracted.staffingRationale: Requires active management and a roster of 6 to 10 employees to operate the retail storefront.

riskReasons: A high-severity operational tripwire is disclosed — see the tripwires section.
notes: Royalty % not found; variable costs may be understated.
Item 17 initial investment not found; debt-service and payback cannot be computed.

underwriting.assessment: Item 17 investment range was not found, so the capital gap cannot be computed from this document.

insights.conceptRationale: Learning Express is a specialty retail toy and gift store concept.

benchmark.label: Retail / product
benchmark.dominantRisk: Gross margin on product (high COGS) and inventory turns. Thin margins amplify any sales miss.
benchmark.rampNote: 3–12 months to run-rate depending on category.
considerations: Budget COGS at 45–65% of sales — product margin is the whole game here.
Verify inventory turns and markdown/shrink rates with Item 20 operators.
Confirm minimum inventory and required reorder commitments in Item 8/Item 11.

crossCheck.message: No operating-EBITDA margin is disclosed in Item 19. The 5–12% band below is an industry estimate for this concept — validate it against real franchisee P&Ls (Item 20) before relying on any margin.

buildup.label: − Labor
buildup.note: conventionally staffed · ≈ 2.1–3.9 FTE at ~$20/hr fully loaded

assumptions.detail: Classified as “Retail / product” from the FDD (Item 1 / brand description).
Taken from the FDD (~$6,729/mo); already netted in margin after fees & rent.
Estimated at the 45–65% category band — COGS is never disclosed in an FDD.
Estimated at the 10–18% conventionally staffed band — labor is never disclosed in an FDD.
Utilities, insurance, and R&M estimated at 6–10% (category catch-all).
Our calculation — the disclosed top line minus the benchmarked costs above. Not a figure stated in the FDD.

insights.consultCtaPitch: These are category benchmarks applied to the disclosed top line — your unit's real costs will differ, and that difference is the conversation. Want them pressure-tested against actual franchisee P&Ls for your territory?
insights.asOf: 2026 — general industry benchmarks, refine against real franchisee P&Ls
insights.disclaimer: Industry benchmark ranges for your own budgeting and Item 20 questions — NOT a projection of this franchise's results, and not investment advice.

financialCondition.headline: The franchisor's statements don't show the distress signals we screen for — positive net worth and a clean audit. Still worth a quick confirmation against current figures.
body: Why it matters to you: field support, training, technology, and marketing come out of the franchisor's budget. If outside funding slows, those are the first things to get cut.

Verify before you commit: ask for the most recent interim (unaudited) financials and the current-year funding plan; ask whether the parent will sign a guarantee of performance; and cross-check Item 20 — unit closures, transfers, and terminations are the operational tell that corroborates financial stress.
mitigants: the auditor issued a clean (unmodified) opinion and did not raise going-concern doubt
financialCondition.evidenceNote: Derived from the Item 21 / Exhibit F audited financials. Figures are extracted; all ratios and grading are computed. Confirm against the source statements and a CPA before relying on them.
